Skin infection with Nocardia species; requires cultures and prolonged treatment.
Clostridial myonecrosis; surgical emergency; requires aggressive debridement.
Severe pneumonia due to Legionella pneumophila; requires Macrolides or Quinolones.
Toxin-mediated multisystem disease; requires ICU support and source control.
Borrelia burgdorferi infection; inpatient needed for IV therapy in neuroborreliosis.
Rickettsia prowazekii infection; requires strict vector control and Tetracyclines.
Rickettsia rickettsii; monitor for vasculitis and hemodynamic instability.
Coxiella burnetii infection; can present as atypical pneumonia or hepatitis.

diagnosis_name: Acute paralytic poliomyelitis, wild virus, imported
notes: Paralytic polio; requires isolation and long-term respiratory and physical rehab.
Rapidly progressive dementia; inpatient for diagnostic workup and supportive care.
Viral encephalitis from wildlife; palliative care and isolation once symptomatic.
Rabies transmitted by domestic dogs; focus on comfort and high-level isolation.
Mosquito-borne viral encephalitis; monitor intracranial pressure and seizures.
Alphavirus infection; supportive care for CNS inflammation.
Highly virulent viral encephalitis; intensive supportive management required.
Flavivirus encephalitis; common in elderly; supportive care for neuro symptoms.
Murray Valley encephalitis; monitor for long-term neurological sequelae.
Cerebral involvement from adenovirus; supportive care and seizure monitoring.
Complication of mumps in males; requires pain management and monitoring.
Viral meningitis from mumps; usually self-limiting but requires observation.
Fungal pneumonia; requires systemic antifungals in severe or progressive cases.
Severe fungal infection in immunocompromised; requires Voriconazole.
Fungal meningitis; requires Amphotericin B and management of intracranial pressure.
Myocardial infarction secondary to ischemia due to either increased oxygen demand or decreased supply; requires management of underlying cause.
Includes MI type 3, 4, and 5; post-procedural or sudden death cases requiring clinical stabilization and observation.

diagnosis_name: Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery with unstable angina pectoris
notes: Known ASHD presenting with worsening angina; requires cardiac monitoring and potential escalation to intervention.

diagnosis_name: Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery with angina pectoris

Asymptomatic history of MI; admission usually for related heart failure symptoms or rhythm monitoring.
Ventricular aneurysm following MI; requires observation for thrombus formation and surgical evaluation.
Dilation of coronary artery; risk of thrombosis or rupture; requires anticoagulation and specialized imaging.
Heart failure resulting from chronic ischemia; requires diuresis and optimization of heart failure therapy.
Objective evidence of ischemia without angina; admission for risk stratification and functional testing.

diagnosis_name: Atherosclerosis of coronary artery bypass graft(s) with unstable angina pectoris
notes: Failure of previous bypass grafts with acute coronary syndrome; high-risk profile for re-intervention.

diagnosis_name: Atherosclerosis of native coronary artery without angina pectoris
notes: Advanced coronary plaque identified during workup for other conditions; elective monitoring for high-risk anatomy.

diagnosis_name: Chronic total occlusion of coronary artery
notes: Long-term blockage; admission for complex percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI).

diagnosis_name: Septic pulmonary embolism with acute cor pulmonale
notes: Infected emboli causing right heart failure; requires long-term IV antibiotics and anticoagulation.

diagnosis_name: Saddle embolus of pulmonary artery with cor pulmonale
notes: Life-threatening large PE at the bifurcation; requires intensive care monitoring and possible thrombolysis.
Idiopathic arterial hypertension of lungs; requires pulmonary vasodilator titration and right heart monitoring.
Cor pulmonale due to chest wall deformity; requires management of respiratory failure and right heart strain.
PAH secondary to connective tissue disease or shunts; requires optimization of underlying condition.
Right ventricular hypertrophy from chronic lung disease; focuses on oxygen therapy and diuresis.
Non-resolved clots causing pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH); workup for surgical endarterectomy.
Ventricular enlargement with reduced EF; focused on acute decompensation management.
Restrictive cardiomyopathy; requires biopsy and management of systemic inflammatory or eosinophilic response.
Stiffening of heart walls; management of diastolic heart failure and rhythm complications.
Toxic cardiomyopathy due to ethanol; requires withdrawal management and HF optimization.

diagnosis_name: Cardiomyopathy due to drugs and other external agents

Non-ischemic myocardial disease not elsewhere classified; requires diagnostic MRI and rhythm monitoring.
Third-degree heart block; requires temporary or permanent pacemaker implantation.
Acute tachyarrhythmia; management with adenosine or synchronized cardioversion.
Malignant arrhythmia; requires antiarrhythmic loading and potential ICD evaluation.
Transient AF with rapid ventricular response; requires rate control and anticoagulation initiation.
Continuous AF >12 months; admission for cardioversion or heart failure management.
Joint decision to stay in AF; admission for rate control optimization or stroke complication.
Macro-reentrant tachycardia; often requires electrophysiology study or cardioversion.
Post-cardiac arrest care; requires neurological assessment and cardiac stabilization.
Stress-induced cardiomyopathy mimicking MI; requires supportive care and LV function monitoring.

diagnosis_name: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of right vertebral artery

diagnosis_name: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of right vertebral artery

diagnosis_name: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of right middle cerebral artery

diagnosis_name: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of right middle cerebral artery

diagnosis_name: Occlusion and stenosis of right carotid artery

diagnosis_name: Occlusion and stenosis of left carotid artery

diagnosis_name: Occlusion and stenosis of right middle cerebral artery

diagnosis_name: Acute embolism and thrombosis of unspecified deep veins of right proximal lower limb

diagnosis_name: Acute embolism and thrombosis of unspecified deep veins of left proximal lower limb

diagnosis_name: Chronic embolism and thrombosis of unspecified deep veins of right proximal lower limb

diagnosis_name: Chronic embolism and thrombosis of unspecified deep veins of left proximal lower limb

diagnosis_name: Acute embolism and thrombosis of deep veins of right upper extremity

diagnosis_name: Acute embolism and thrombosis of right internal jugular vein

diagnosis_name: Hypertension secondary to other renal disorders

diagnosis_name: Acute appendicitis with localized peritonitis, unspecified

Surgical management for appendiceal diverticulosis to prevent future perforation.
Complex surgical repair; monitor for enteric leak and ensure proper bowel function.

diagnosis_name: Calculus of gallbladder with other cholecystitis

diagnosis_name: Calculus of gallbladder with other cholecystitis, with obstruction

diagnosis_name: Calculus of bile duct with cholangitis, acute, without obstruction

diagnosis_name: Calculus of bile duct with cholangitis, acute, with obstruction

diagnosis_name: Calculus of bile duct with cholangitis, chronic, without obstruction

diagnosis_name: Calculus of both gallbladder and bile duct with cholecystitis, unspecified

diagnosis_name: Calculus of both gallbladder and bile duct with cholecystitis, with obstruction

diagnosis_name: Calculus of both gallbladder and bile duct with acute cholecystitis, unspecified

Complex surgery for cholecystoenteric fistula; monitor for bowel obstruction.
Surgical removal if symptomatic (strawberry gallbladder).

diagnosis_name: Bilateral inguinal hernia, with obstruction, without gangrene, not specified as recurrent

diagnosis_name: Unilateral inguinal hernia, with obstruction, without gangrene, recurrent

diagnosis_name: Unilateral inguinal hernia, without obstruction or gangrene, recurrent

diagnosis_name: Bilateral femoral hernia, with obstruction, without gangrene, not specified as recurrent

diagnosis_name: Diaphragmatic hernia with obstruction, without gangrene

Ruptured ectopic pregnancy in other locations (e.g., cervical, interstitial or cornual).

Inertia of the uterus not otherwise specified during delivery.

Miscellaneous contractile disorders affecting labor progress.
Undifferentiated uterine dysfunction during delivery process.

diagnosis_name: Newborn affected by maternal anesthesia and analgesia

Workup for mixed etiology respiratory symptoms in the immediate postnatal period.

Investigation of cardiac versus respiratory cause for transient oxygen desaturations.
Critical event requiring stabilization, resuscitation, and workup for neurological or metabolic cause.

Pylorectomy and lymph node dissection for localized gastric outlet tumor.

Appendectomy for carcinoid or appendiceal adenocarcinoma.

Non-anatomical liver resection for specified primary liver malignancies.
Hemihepatectomy for unspecified liver primary malignancies.

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of overlapping sites of pancreas

Pneumonectomy for central lung malignancy near main bronchus.

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of bronchus or lung, unspecified

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of overlapping sites of bone and articular cartilage of limbs

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of bone and articular cartilage of limbs, unspecified

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of skin of lip

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of skin of scalp and neck

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of skin of trunk

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of skin of lower limb, including hip

diagnosis_name: Malignant neoplasm of cervix uteri, unspecified
